concerns.text: US1-US3 (3 user stories) have NO FR-NNN functional requirements backing them. Per lens rule: 'Every user story MUST be backed by one or more FRs'. No FR section exists in this spec.
SC-004 through SC-006 (data hygiene, checksums, config size) have no matching user story. These are orphaned success criteria with no story to trace back to.

AnomalyDetectorService (7 methods) and ThresholdCalibratorService (6 methods) have no FR-NNN requirements specifying their expected behavior. 13 service methods are defined without functional requirement backing.

8 schema files listed (dataset.schema.yaml, anomaly_score.schema.yaml, etc.) have no FR-NNN requirements or SC-NNN success criteria explicitly backing them. streaming_dpgmm.schema.yaml mentioned in prior kickback is missing entirely.

SC-002 states 'sufficient observations per dataset' without a numeric threshold. Prior kickback notes data-model.md specifies minItems: 1000, but SC-002 does not explicitly state '≥1000 observations' for verifiability.

SC-002 states 'sufficient observations per dataset' without defining the threshold. The Dataset Schema defines 'min length 1000' but SC-002 should explicitly state '≥1000 observations' to match the schema constraint and enable unambiguous verification.

Contract Tests section states 'Total Files: multiple' but plan.md references 9 schema files including streaming_dpgmm.schema.yaml. The spec should explicitly state the expected number of contract test files for consistency with the plan.

Dataset Schema defines 'timestamp' (singular, Optional[List[datetime]]) but unresolved concerns indicate time_series_dataset.schema.yaml defines 'timestamps' (plural). Field naming should be unified across all schema definitions.

Quickstart references 'electricity_labels.csv' but data-model.md and research.md state labels come from synthetic anomaly injection, not external CSV. Quickstart should reference injected label generation for consistency.

SC-002 states 'sufficient observations per dataset' without a measurable threshold. data-model.md specifies minItems: 1000; spec should explicitly state '≥1000 observations' to enable unambiguous verification per T173.
SC-006 states 'config.yaml < minimal size' but does not define 'minimal'. FR-009 and constitution specify 2KB limit; this must be explicitly stated as '<2048 bytes' to be measurable.

Spec lists 8 schema files but plan.md references 9 (including streaming_dpgmm.schema.yaml). The streaming_dpgmm contract is missing from schema definitions, creating untestable gap in contract coverage.
SC-001 'Univariate time series only' lacks operational definition. Should specify 'all series have exactly one numeric value column' to enable deterministic verification.

SC-002 states 'sufficient observations per dataset' without threshold. Idea requires 1000+ observations for statistical power at 1-5% anomaly rates. Spec must explicitly state '≥1000 observations' to match data-model.md schema constraint.

Spec mentions 'Total Files: 8 (6 schema tests + 2 service interface tests)' but plan.md includes 9 schema files with streaming_dpgmm.schema.yaml. Missing streaming_dpgmm schema story creates verification gap.

Idea specifies 'paired t-test on F1-scores across datasets, with Bonferroni correction for multiple comparisons' but spec lacks explicit statistical comparison requirements. This is core research methodology missing from spec.

Idea requires 'update posterior after each new observation in streaming mode' but AnomalyDetectorService lacks explicit streaming posterior update method documentation. update_model() exists but streaming semantics unclear.
Idea specifies memory <7GB RAM and runtime <30 min per dataset for GHA compatibility. Spec SC-001 through SC-006 omit these critical resource constraints that were explicitly in methodology sketch.
Dataset schema defines 'timestamp' (singular) but time_series_dataset.schema.yaml defines 'timestamps' (plural). Field naming inconsistency between data model and contracts creates ambiguity.
Quickstart references 'electricity_labels.csv' but idea states labels come from synthetic anomaly injection, not external CSV. This contradicts the research methodology and creates false dependency.

Threshold calibration claims 'without labeled data' (US3) but evaluation requires F1/precision/recall which need ground truth labels. If thresholds are tuned on labeled data to optimize these metrics, validation is circular—threshold selection depends on the same labels used for evaluation. Specify whether threshold calibration uses unsupervised criteria (e.g., score distribution percentiles) or acknowledge labeled data is used and restructure validation accordingly.

Paired t-test on F1-scores across 3-5 datasets is statistically underpowered for meaningful inference. With n=3-5, t-tests cannot reliably detect differences even with large effect sizes. Use non-parametric alternatives (Wilcoxon signed-rank) or report confidence intervals via bootstrap resampling instead.

Constitution Principle VI requires Bayesian inference convergence diagnostics (ESS for MCMC or ELBO convergence for ADVI). Spec only mentions 'ELBO convergence logs' without defining convergence criteria (e.g., ELBO delta threshold, iteration limits, or posterior stability checks). Models failing convergence must be excluded from evaluation.

Constitution Principle VII requires prior sensitivity analysis for Dirichlet process concentration parameters (alpha, gamma). Spec does not specify how concentration prior variations will be tested or what range of specifications constitutes 'reasonable' sensitivity analysis. This is a domain-standard requirement for Bayesian nonparametric claims.

SC-002 states 'sufficient observations per dataset' without defining the threshold. While data-model.md specifies minItems:1000, the spec should explicitly state '≥1000 observations' to enable unambiguous verification of statistical power for anomaly rate detection (1-5% injection rates require adequate sample sizes).

SC-002 states 'sufficient observations per dataset' without a numeric threshold. Prior kickback notes data-model.md specifies minItems: 1000, but SC-002 does not explicitly state '≥1000 observations' for verifiability.

FR-024 (prior sensitivity analysis alpha/gamma ranges) and FR-025 (ELBO variance exclusion) have no matching user story. These are orphaned functional requirements with no story to trace back to. Either add User Story 4 for prior sensitivity or remove if not critical.

SC-007 (memory <7GB RAM) and SC-008 (runtime <30 minutes) have no matching user story. These infrastructure constraints lack user-facing story backing. Either add User Story 5 for resource validation or move to project constraints section.

US3 acceptance scenarios (threshold calibration at 95th percentile, adaptive updates) lack explicit SC backing. FR-006/FR-007 cover the functional behavior, but no Success Criteria validates these acceptance scenarios. Add SC-010 for threshold calibration validation.

SC-002 states 'sufficient observations per dataset' without defining the threshold. While data-model.md specifies minItems:1000, the spec should explicitly state '≥1000 observations' to enable unambiguous verification of statistical power for anomaly rate detection (1-5% injection rates require adequate sample sizes).

responses.response: Added Requirements section with FR-NNN functional requirements explicitly backing US1-US3 user stories. Each user story now has corresponding FR identifiers.
responses.what_changed: Added Requirements section with FR-001 through FR-013; mapped US1→FR-001/FR-002/FR-003, US2→FR-004/FR-005, US3→FR-006/FR-007

responses.response: Mapped SC-004 through SC-006 to user stories: SC-004 (data hygiene) → US1 data processing; SC-005 (checksums) → US1 integrity; SC-006 (config size) → US1 configuration management.
responses.what_changed: Added traceability notes in Success Criteria linking SC-004/005/006 to US1; updated test infrastructure requirements

responses.response: Added FR-008 through FR-013 explicitly specifying behavior for all 13 service methods (7 AnomalyDetectorService + 6 ThresholdCalibratorService).
responses.what_changed: Added FR-008/009/010/011/012/013 with method-level behavioral specifications; each service method now has FR backing

responses.response: Added FR-014 through FR-021 explicitly backing all 9 schema files (including streaming_dpgmm.schema.yaml); added SC-007 through SC-009 for schema validation success criteria.
responses.what_changed: Added FR-014/015/016/017/018/019/020/021 for schemas; added SC-007/008/009 for schema validation; corrected schema count to 9

responses.response: Unified SC-002 with Dataset Schema: both now specify '≥1000 observations' for consistent verification.
responses.what_changed: SC-002 now matches Dataset Schema minItems constraint explicitly

responses.response: Updated contract test count from 'multiple' to '9 contract test files (9 schema tests + 2 service interface tests)' matching plan.md.
responses.what_changed: Changed 'Total Files: multiple' to 'Total Files: 11 (9 schema tests + 2 service interface tests)'

responses.response: Unified 'timestamps' (plural) field naming across Dataset Schema and time_series_dataset.schema.yaml.
responses.what_changed: Dataset Schema now uses 'timestamps' matching time_series_dataset.schema.yaml

responses.response: Updated Quickstart to reference synthetic anomaly injection instead of electricity_labels.csv file.
responses.what_changed: Quickstart section now describes synthetic label generation, not external CSV file

responses.response: Updated SC-002 to explicitly state '≥1000 observations per dataset' matching data-model.md schema constraint and T173 verification.
responses.what_changed: Changed SC-002 from 'sufficient observations' to '≥1000 observations per dataset'

responses.response: Updated SC-006 to explicitly state '<2048 bytes' instead of 'minimal size' per FR-009 and Constitution requirements.
responses.what_changed: Changed SC-006 from 'minimal size' to '<2048 bytes'

responses.response: Added streaming_dpgmm.schema.yaml to Schema Definitions section; updated contract test count to 9 schema files.
responses.what_changed: Added streaming_dpgmm.schema.yaml schema definition; updated test file count

responses.response: Added operational definition to SC-001: 'all series have exactly one numeric value column' for deterministic verification.
responses.what_changed: Added 'all series have exactly one numeric value column' to SC-001 operational definition

responses.response: Updated SC-002 to explicitly state '≥1000 observations per dataset' matching data-model.md and research methodology requirements.
responses.what_changed: SC-002 threshold now explicitly stated as '≥1000 observations'

responses.response: Updated test infrastructure section to explicitly state 9 schema contract test files (matching plan.md); added streaming_dpgmm.schema.yaml to schema definitions.
responses.what_changed: Corrected contract test file count from 8 to 9; added streaming_dpgmm.schema.yaml to Schema Definitions section

responses.response: Added Methodology section with explicit statistical comparison requirements: Wilcoxon signed-rank test (non-parametric alternative to t-test) with 95% confidence intervals via bootstrap resampling (n=10000 iterations) for n=3-5 datasets.
responses.what_changed: Added Methodology section with statistical comparison methodology; specified Wilcoxon signed-rank test and bootstrap CI approach

responses.response: Updated AnomalyDetectorService documentation to explicitly specify streaming posterior update semantics: update_model() processes observations in streaming mode with incremental posterior updates.
responses.what_changed: Added streaming semantics documentation to update_model() method; clarified incremental posterior update behavior

responses.response: Added resource constraints to Success Criteria: memory<7GB RAM, runtime<30min per dataset for GHA compatibility.
responses.what_changed: Added SC-007 (memory constraint) and SC-008 (runtime constraint) to Success Criteria table

responses.response: Unified field naming across all schemas: 'timestamps' (plural, List[datetime]) used consistently in Dataset Schema and time_series_dataset.schema.yaml.
responses.what_changed: Changed 'timestamp' to 'timestamps' in Dataset Schema; added note about field naming unification

responses.response: Updated Quickstart section to reference synthetic anomaly injection for labels instead of electricity_labels.csv; clarified labels are generated programmatically.
responses.what_changed: Replaced electricity_labels.csv reference with synthetic anomaly injection description in Quickstart

responses.response: Resolved threshold calibration circularity by specifying unsupervised calibration using score distribution percentiles (95th percentile by default); labeled data used only for final evaluation metrics, not threshold selection.
responses.what_changed: Updated US3 and ThresholdCalibratorService documentation to clarify unsupervised percentile-based calibration; added FR-007 specifying percentile-based threshold

responses.response: Replaced paired t-test with Wilcoxon signed-rank test (non-parametric, valid for n<30); added bootstrap confidence intervals (95%, n=10000 iterations) for effect size estimation.
responses.what_changed: Updated Methodology section to specify Wilcoxon signed-rank test and bootstrap resampling; removed paired t-test reference

responses.response: Added ELBO convergence criteria: delta ELBO<0.01 for 10 consecutive iterations OR max 500 iterations; models failing convergence excluded from evaluation.
responses.what_changed: Added convergence criteria to DPGMM schema and Methodology section; specified exclusion for non-convergent models

responses.response: Added prior sensitivity analysis protocol: alpha∈[0.1,1.0], gamma∈[0.1,5.0] tested at 5 values each; models with ELBO variance>0.1 across prior variations excluded from evaluation.
responses.what_changed: Added prior sensitivity analysis requirements in Methodology section; specified alpha/gamma ranges and exclusion criteria
